frameworks.name: diffusion tensor imaging
frameworks.one_line: Use DTI fiber-orientation metrics of corticospinal tract to predict TMS motor threshold.

frameworks.name: linear regression model
frameworks.one_line: Two structural parameters predict motor threshold variance across subjects.

core_question: Which structural parameters of the corticospinal tract and skull-cortex geometry predict TMS motor threshold?
key_assumptions: Corticospinal tract fiber orientation modulates TMS-induced current
Skull-to-cortex distance attenuates the induced electric field
Fractional anisotropy and demographic factors are candidate but not necessary predictors
manipulations: Measured fractional anisotropy in CST ROIs
Measured principal diffusion direction (anterior-posterior trajectory) in CST
Measured skull-to-cortex distance over left motor region
Included with and without schizophrenia subjects
key_findings: Anterior-posterior trajectory of CST principal diffusion direction correlated with motor threshold
Skull-to-cortex distance correlated with motor threshold
Fractional anisotropy, age, and schizophrenia status not correlated with motor threshold
Two-parameter linear model accounts for 82% of motor-threshold variance
interpretations: Fiber orientation at the internal capsule and skull-cortex distance jointly explain individual TMS excitability
